apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.introduct: Welcome to the tutorial for Apothic World Tiers! In this tutorial, you will learn about the new World Tier system, and how to progress through it. Click "Next" to continue, or click "Skip Tutorial" to skip this tutorial.
apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.world_tie:
World Tiers are the progression system for Apotheosis. Unlocking and activating new World Tiers will raise the difficulty of the game, but also increase the quality of loot that you find in the world.

Hover over a World Tier's icon to see the unlock requirements, and click it to focus an unlocked one.
apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.tier_name: This is the name of the selected World Tier, as well as a short description of the tier.
apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.tier_diff:
This is the difficulty of the selected World Tier. Higher difficulties mean stronger mobs, but also better rewards.

At World Tier: Frontier and above, Apothic Invaders will begin to spawn in your world.
apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.detailed_:
This button will show you detailed information about the World Tier, including the buffs and debuffs for both players and mobs, as well as the drop chances for each rarity and gem purity.

If you aren't sure what Rarities or Purities are, see the Chronicle of Shadows (the in-game guidebook).
This button allows you to activate the selected world tier, if it is not already active.

Clicking "Done" will automatically activate World Tier: Haven. For all other World Tiers, you need to return here and activate them yourself.
apotheosis.tutorial.apotheosis.world_tier.activate_:
Normally, this button allows you to activate the selected world tier.

However, in this modpack, World Tiers are automatically activated and cannot be manually changed. You can still view the details of each World Tier, but you cannot activate or deactivate them.

aquaculture.aquaculture.message1: How do I mine for fish?
aquaculture.aquaculture.message2: Doctor said I should start bottling up my feelings.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message3: Help I'm trapped in a Message In A Bottle making factory!
aquaculture.aquaculture.message4: My house was griefed, send supplies!
aquaculture.aquaculture.message5: Hey! Over here! I think I see you!
aquaculture.aquaculture.message8: Warning: Choking Hazard, keep out of reach of children.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message9: If you get this, this is you from the future, do NOT eat the porkchop.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message12: Neptunite is REAL, I tell you! I wasn't making it up!
aquaculture.aquaculture.message13: Enlarge your Fishing Rod for only 19.95 Gold! Guaranteed!
aquaculture.aquaculture.message14: What the hell is Fishallurgy? Some sort of reaction to fish?
aquaculture.aquaculture.message15: WHALE BURGERS! 2x Bread + Whale Steak! Remember those!?
aquaculture.aquaculture.message16: Ever since I came ashore things haven't been the same, because the moment I stepped onto the shore I totally lost the game..
aquaculture.aquaculture.message18: Oh I've got a lovely bunch of coconuts..
aquaculture.aquaculture.message19: I joined a tribe of cannibals to meat new people.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message21: Dear Shadowclaimer, add Unobtainium Hyper Diamonds that are invulnerable with instant mining speed tools and armor. Sincerely, Everyone.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message22: Dear Shadowclaimer, so when's 1.X coming out? Sincerely, Everyone.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message24: The tunaverse is truly an incredible place
aquaculture.aquaculture.message25: Null Pointer Exception: Aquaculture.MessageInABottle (String) cannot be null.
aquaculture.aquaculture.message26: I grind up diamond armor every morning and eat it for fast. - mDiyo
aquaculture.aquaculture.message27: -The message magically fades from your hand.-
aquaculture.aquaculture.message28: Look up at me, now back at your inventory, now back at me, this message is now DIAMONDS! Nah just kidding, its still paper.

Affects how common artifacts are in chests
Values above 1 will make artifacts rarer, values between 0 and 1 will make artifacts more common
Doubling this value will make artifacts approximately twice as hard to find, and vice versa
To prevent artifacts from appearing as chest loot, set this to 10000.

constructionstick.advancement.constructionstick.root.title: Construction Sticks
constructionstick.advancement.constructionstick.template_r: Out with the old, in with the new
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.AngelFal: Place blocks below you while falling > 10 blocks with angel upgrade (Can be used to save you from drops/the void)
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.BEList.t: White/Blacklist for Block Entities. Allow/Prevent blocks with BEs from being placed by stick. You can either add block ids like minecraft:chest or mod ids like minecraft
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.BEWhitel: If set to TRUE, treat BEList as a whitelist, otherwise blacklist
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.MaxRange: Maximum placement range (0: unlimited). Affects all sticks and is meant for lag prevention, not game balancing.
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.SimilarB: Blocks to treat equally when in Similar mode. Enter block IDs separated by ;
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.UndoHist: Number of operations that can be undone
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.angel.to: Stick destruction block limit (0 to disable destruction upgrade)
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.destruct: Stick destruction block limit (0 to disable destruction upgrade)
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.limit.to: Max placement distance with angel upgrade (0 to disable angel upgrade)
constructionstick.constructionstick.configuration.upgradea: Allow stick upgrading by putting the stick together with a stick upgrade in a smithing table.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.stick:
The %s can place up to %s blocks at the side of a building facing you and lasts %s.

Press the bound %s key to change placement restriction (Horizontal, Vertical, North/South, East/West, No lock).

Open the option screen with the bound %s key.

§5§nUNDO§0§r
Holding down the bound %s key while looking at a blocks will show you the last blocks you placed with a green border around them. Pressing the bound %s key while looking at any of them will undo the operation, giving you all the items back. If you used the Destruction upgrade, it will restore the blocks.

§5§nCONTAINERS§0§r
Shulker boxes, bundles and many containers from other mods can provide building blocks for the stick.

§5§nOFFHAND PRIORITY§0§r
Having blocks in your offhand will place them instead of the block you're looking at.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.template_a: The angel upgrade places a block on the opposite side of the block (or row of blocks) you are facing. Maximum distance depends on stick tier. Right click empty space to place a block in midair. To do that, you'll need to have the block you want to place in your offhand.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.template_b: The battery upgrade allows you to use energy instead of durability. The stick will have the ability to store energy and use it to place blocks. Warning, you can only charge the stick if you have a mod that provides a way to do so.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.template_d: The destruction upgrade destroys blocks (no block entities) on the side facing you. Maximum number of blocks depends on stick tier. Destroyed blocks disappear into the void, you can use the undo feature if you've made a mistake.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.template_r: The replacement upgrade allows you to exchange blocks with the block of the type you are holding in your offhand. This can be useful for replacing walls after placement.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.template_u: The unbreakable upgrade allows you to place blocks without using durability. The stick will never break.
constructionstick.constructionstick.description.upgrade:
§5§nINSTALLATION§0§r
Put your new upgrade template together with your stick and the required item in a Smithing Table to apply (Check the Smithing Table recipes to see the required item). To switch between upgrades, press the bound %s key while holding your stick or use the option screen.
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.direction.targe: Place blocks with same direction as target block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.lock.eastwest.d: Build a row in E/W direction on top of the original block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.lock.horizontal: Build a horizontal column in front of the original block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.lock.nolock.des: Extend from any side of the original block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.lock.northsouth: Build a row in N/S direction on top of the original block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.lock.vertical.d: Build a vertical column in front of the original block
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.match.exact.des: Only extend blocks that are exactly the same
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.random.yes.desc: Place random blocks present in your hotbar
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.replace.yes.des: Replace certain blocks like fluids, snow and tallgrass
constructionstick.constructionstick.option.upgrades.constr: Extend your building on the side facing you
Place behind blocks and in mid air
Destroys blocks on the side facing you
Replaces blocks with the block in your offhand
constructionstick.constructionstick.placement.denied: This block cannot be placed with the Construction Stick!

darkmodeeverywhere.config.darkmodeeverywhere.method_shader_:
A list of class:method strings (render methods) that the dark shader will not be applied to.
Each string consists of the class and the method (or any substring) to block the dark shader.
For example, 'renderHunger' is sufficient to block 'net.minecraftforge.client.gui.overlay.ForgeGui:renderFood' (either will work)

Enabling this config will (every 5 seconds) dump which methods were used to render GUIs that the dark shader was applied to
The dump will consist of a list of class:method strings, e.g. 'net.minecraftforge.client.gui.overlay.ForgeGui:renderFood'
Use this feature to help find the render method strings of GUIs you would like to blacklist.

extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.name: Extended Crafting
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.landing_text: Extended Crafting adds new ways to craft items, as well as extra crafting items and utilities. $(br2)This guide book is for explaining the functionality of the different crafting methods in this mod. How they are implemented is decided by the modpack creator.
This section goes over the different crafting mechanisms added by Extended Crafting.
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.category.component: This section goes over the different crafting items added by Extended Crafting.
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.category.utilities: This section goes over the different crafting utilities added by Extended Crafting.
Combination Crafting is an in-world multi-block crafting setup where items are combined together to create something $(italic)special.$() $(br2)This setup consists of a Crafting Core in the center and pedestals placed around it. Pedestals can be placed anywhere around the Crafting Core in a 3 block radius, and must be on the same Y level.

Power must be provided to the Crafting Core for this structure to function. $(br2)Recipes have a center item which goes on the Crafting Core, and pedestal items which go on the pedestals around it. $(br)Right clicking on the side of the Crafting Core will show you information about the current crafting operation, such as progress or power cost, as well as how much power is stored in it.
This mod adds tiered crafting tables that come in $(bold)3x3$(), $(bold)5x5$(), $(bold)7x7$(), and $(bold)9x9$() sizes. These tables hold their inventories when closed.
Quantum Compression is the process of compressing items a little too much. This is done using a Quantum Compressor. $(br2)The Quantum Compressor is a powered machine that can hold and compress extremely large amounts of materials. Items placed inside the Quantum Compressor go into an internal buffer for use in compression. The buffer is represented as a blue bar.

Each recipe $(bold)usually$() requires a catalyst. The catalyst is placed in the left-most slot and does not get used up. $(br2)Once there are enough items in the buffer, the machine will begin to craft the item. The Quantum Compressor will only use power while crafting. $(br2)If you happen to put items into the compressor that you would rather not lose forever, you can press the eject button above the input slot to get them back.

Items in the buffer are stored in the endless void and can only be re-obtained by ejecting. Breaking the block will cause those items to get lost forever, so be careful! $(br2)The item input buffer of the Quantum Compressor is capable of holding $(bold)billions$() of items, but by default will only hold as many as the recipe requires. You can disable this limit by clicking on the lock button next to the input buffer.
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.entry.ender_crafti: Ender Crafting is crafting using the power of waiting. An Ender Crafting setup consists of an Ender Crafter and Ender Alternators. The crafter will take energy from the void via the alternators. The more alternators you have, the faster it will craft.
Ender Alternators can be placed anywhere around the Ender Crafter in a $(bold)3 block radius$(). Even above or below!
Auto Crafting Tables are an upgraded version of $(l:crafting/tables)Crafting Tables$() that can automatically insert items and be piped out of. $(br2)Item insertion is done by simply placing a chest above the table, and inserting items into said chest. Item insertion uses power.

For item insertion to work, you must lay out a recipe in the grid and save the recipe. This is done by shift+left clicking on one of the 3 recipe slots in the top right. You can then select the recipe by clicking on it. You can delete the recipe by shift+left clicking it. $(br2)Items can be extracted from the table by a hopper or any other compatible item extraction mechanism.
You can disable auto crafting by clicking on the down arrow under the output slot.
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.entry.flux_craftin: Flux Crafting is crafting using both the power of waiting, and the power of energy. A Flux Crafting setup consists of a Flux Crafter and Flux Alternators. The crafter will take energy from the alternators. The more alternators you have, the faster it will craft.
Flux Alternators can be placed anywhere around the Flux Crafter in a $(bold)3 block radius$(). Even above or below!
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.entry.singularitie: Singularities are highly compressed materials. These are $(bold)usually$() made using a $(l:crafting/compression)Quantum Compressor$(). $(br2)Singularities can $(bold)probably$() be used for many things, such as $(l:components/ultimate_singularity)The Ultimate Singularity$().
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.entry.ultimate_sin: The Ultimate Singularity is $(bold)usually$() made in the $(l:crafting/tables)Ultimate Crafting Table$(). $(br2)It is $(bold)usually$() made of all, if not most, of the available singularities.
extendedcrafting.book.extendedcrafting.entry.handheld_tab: The Handheld Crafting Table is a Crafting Table.

ftbchunks.ftbchunks.advanced.memory.region_release:
Timeout in seconds to release data for 512x512 regions which haven't been accessed recently
Smaller values mean less memory usage, but more disk access as released regions are reloaded
Set to 0 to disable releasing of region data.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.advanced.memory.autorelease_on:
When the large map is closed, auto-release region data down to this number.
Smaller values mean less memory usage, but more disk access as released regions are reloaded
Set to 0 to disable releasing of region data.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.advanced.memory.max_zoom_const:
When true, max map zoom-out is limited by the number of explored regions and amount of available memory.

ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_fake_play:
Treat ALL fake players as allies of the team.
WARNING: Setting this to true could allow hostile players to access your claims via any fake player. Set this to false if you're unsure.
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_named_fak:
Treat these fake players as allies of the team.
WARNING: Adding entries here could allow hostile players to access your claims via those fake players. Leave this empty if you're unsure.
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_fake_play: Allow Fake Players by Player ID
Allows fake players which have the ID of a real player access to your claims, IF that real player would be permitted, either as ally or team member. Set this to true if you're unsure.
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_explosion: Should explosions be able to damage blocks in claimed areas?
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_pvp.toolt:
Should player-vs-player combat be allowed in claimed areas?
Server config setting 'Allow PvP Combat' must be 'per_team' for this to function
Not guaranteed to protect against 100%% of indirect attacks; requires that damage sources can be attributed to a player
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.allow_mob_grief:
Should mobs be allowed to damage blocks in claimed areas?
Note: currently Endermen only; may include other mobs in future
Creeper explosions are protected against via "Allow Explosions"
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.block_edit_and_: Used when blocks are being placed, broken, or interacted with
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.block_edit_mode: Used when blocks are being placed or broken
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.block_interact_: Used when blocks are right-clicked, e.g. opening a chest or flipping a lever
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.nonliving_entit: Used when non-living entities (paintings, item frames etc.) are left-clicked
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.location_mode.t: Controls who can see you on the map or minimap (outside the normal vanilla tracking range)
ftbchunks.ftbteamsconfig.ftbchunks.claim_visibilit: Controls who can see your claims on the map or minimap
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.fake_players.fake_players.tool:
CHECK: check fake player access like any real player
DENY: never allow fake players
ALLOW: always allow fake players
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.max_claimed_chunks.to: This default can be overridden by the FTB Ranks 'ftbchunks.max_claimed` permission node.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.force_loading.max_force_loaded: This default can be overridden by the FTB Ranks 'ftbchunks.max_force_loaded` permission node.
NEVER: only allow chunk force-loading if the owning team has at least one online player
ALWAYS: always allow force-loading, even if no players are online
DEFAULT: allow force-loading IF the team has at least one player with the FTB Ranks 'ftbchunks.chunk_load_offline' permission
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.disable_protection.tooltip: Useful for private servers where everyone is trusted, and claims are only used for force-loading
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.pvp_mode: Allow PvP Combat in Claimed Chunks
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.pvp_mode.tooltip:
ALWAYS: allow PvP combat in all claimed chunks
NEVER: prevent PvP in all claimed chunks
PER_TEAM: teams can decide if PvP is allowed in their claims via team property
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.ally_mode: Allow Player to Change Ally Settings
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.ally_mode.tooltip:
DEFAULT: FTB Teams ally status is checked to decide if players are allied
FORCED_ALL: all players are always considered to be allied
FORCED_NONE: no players are ever considered to be allied
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.claim_dimension_black:
Blacklist for dimension ID's where chunks may not be claimed.
E.g. add "minecraft:the_end" to this list if you want to disable chunk claiming in The End
Wildcards are allowed, e.g. "othermod:*" matches all dimensions added by "othermod"
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.claim_dimension_white:
Whitelist for dimension ID's where chunks may be claimed. If non-empty, dimension *must* be in this list (and also not in "Dimension Blacklist".
Same syntax as for "Dimension Blacklist"
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.no_wilderness.tooltip: If true, chunks must be claimed before they can be built on
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.no_wilderness_dimensions.toolt:
List of dimension ID's where chunks must be claimed before modifying.
E.g. add "minecraft:the_nether" to require chunks to be claimed in the Nether.
Wildcards are allowed, e.g. "othermod:*" matches all dimensions added by "othermod"
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.max_idle_days_before_:
If no team member logs in for this many days, the team's claims will be released.
Setting this to 0 disables auto-unclaiming.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.force_loading.max_idle_days_be:
If no team member logs in for this many days, any force-loaded chunks will no longer be force-loaded.
Setting this to 0 disables auto-unforceloading.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.long_range_tracker_interval.to:
Interval in ticks to send updates to clients with long-range player tracking data.
Lower values mean more frequent updates but more server load and network traffic; be careful with this, especially on busy servers.
Setting this to 0 disables long-range tracking.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.protect_unknown_explosions.too:
Some explosion sources (e.g. Ghasts) can't be determined in code.
If this setting is true, damage from these explosion is prevented in protected chunks.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.hard_team_claim_limit:
Hard claim limit for party teams, regardless of member count or Party Limit Calculation mode.
Default of 0 means no hard limit.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.force_loading.hard_team_force_:
Hard force-load limit for party teams, regardless of member count or Party Limit Calculation mode.
Default of 0 means no hard limit.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.claiming.party_limit_mode.tool:
Method by which party claim & force-load limits are calculated.
LARGEST: use the limits of the member with the largest limits
SUM: add up all the members' limits
OWNER: use the party owner's limits only
AVERAGE: use the average of all members' limits.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.require_game_stage.tooltip: If true, players must have the 'ftbchunks_mapping' game stage (KubeJS and/or Gamestages required) to be able to open the map or see the minimap
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.location_mode_override.tooltip: If true, team "Location Visibility" settings are ignored, and all players can see each other anywhere on the map
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.piston_protection.tooltip:
If true, pistons may not push/pull blocks across claims owned by different teams (unless the block is entering/leaving a claim with block edit mode set to Public)
If 'disable_protection' is set to true, this setting is ignored.
ftbchunks.ftbchunks.fake_players.max_prevented_log: Fake Player Prevented Access Log Age

Age in days to keep logs of prevented fake player access
Note: not fully implemented feature; will be used in future to display & control access by fake players to your claims

ftbfiltersystem.item.ftbfiltersystem.smart_filter.toolti: Sneak + Right-click: Test filter with item in off hand
ftbfiltersystem.ftbfiltersystem.message.not_a_filter: You must hold a Smart Filter in your main hand
ftbfiltersystem.ftbfiltersystem.message.not_configured: This Smart Filter has no filter configured
ftbfiltersystem.ftbfiltersystem.message.no_offhand_item: Hold an item to be tested in your off hand
ftbfiltersystem.ftbfiltersystem.gui.changes_made: You have made changes to this filter
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.and.name: All Of
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.and.tooltip: Compound filter: this filter matches if ALL of its child filters match.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.or.name: Any Of
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.or.tooltip: Compound filter: this filter matches if ANY of its child filters match.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.not.name: Not
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.not.tooltip: Compound filter: this filter matches if its child filter does NOT match. At most one child can be added.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.only_one.name: Only One Of
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.only_one.tooltip: Compound filter: this filter matches if exactly ONE of its child filters matches.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.block.name: Is Block
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.block.tooltip: Simple filter which matches if the item is a placeable block.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.component.name: Item Component
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.component.tooltip:
Matches item data components on an item.
Supports exact (all components must match) and fuzzy matches (only match components in the filter).
If you have admin permissions, items in your inventory which have (non-default) component data can be clicked to copy their current data into the text editor, serialized as SNBT.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.durability.name: Durability
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.durability.toolti:
Matches an item based on its remaining durability.
Non-damageable items are considered to have a durability of 0.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.food_value.name: Food Value
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.food_value.toolti:
Matches an item based on its nutritional value (half-shanks of food restored)
Non-food items have a nutritional value of 0
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.item.name: Item
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.item.tooltip:
Matches against a specific item.
Item component data is not checked here (see Component Filter)
Items can be picked from Creative or Inventory item lists.
Use the textfield to filter item ID's to limit the displayed items.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.item_tag.name: Item Tag
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.item_tag.tooltip:
Matches against an item tag; items which are in the tag will be matched.
Use the textfield to filter displayed item tags.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.stack_size.name: Stack Size
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.stack_size.toolti: Matches against an item's current stack size.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.max_stack_size.to:
Matches against an item's maximum stack size.
Unstackable items have a maximum stack size of 1.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.mod.name: Mod
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.mod.tooltip:
Matches against the mod which adds the item.
Vanilla items are in the Minecraft 'mod'.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.custom.name: Custom
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.custom.tooltip:
Advanced filter intended for modders and modpack makers.
Fires a CustomFilterEvent when a match is attempted; set the event result to indicate a successful or failed match.
Use the textfield to provide freeform text data which is passed to the event as the 'data' parameter.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.expression.name: Expression
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.expression.toolti:
Allows a literal filter expression to be entered or loaded from a Filter item in your inventory.
It is optional (but recommended!) to assign a custom name, for clarity.
ftbfiltersystem.filter.ftbfiltersystem.root.name: Root (All Of)

ftbquests.ftbquests.file.detection_delay.tooltip:
Minimum interval (in ticks) between checks of a player's inventory when it changes
Higher values are kinder on server TPS, especially if players inventories change frequently, but mean longer delays for quest auto-completion messages.

ftbquests.ftbquests.chapter.image.hover.tooltip: You can use literal text or translation keys here
ftbquests.ftbquests.chapter.image.click.tooltip:
Provide custom functionality when the image is clicked:
• Open a page or file with http://, https:// or file:/// URL
• Run a command with 'command:<cmd>'
• Jump to a quest with '#<quest_id>' (use Copy ID to get quest id's)
• Fire a CustomClickEvent (for KubeJS or other mods) with 'custom:<resource_location>'
ftbquests.ftbquests.chapter.appearance.default_min: Default Min Opened Quest Window Width
ftbquests.ftbquests.chapter.visibility.hide_text_u: Hide Text Until Quest is Completed
ftbquests.ftbquests.quest.cannot_copy_many: Copying multiple objects is not supported!

ftbquests.ftbquests.quest.misc.exclusive.desc: Completing this makes other branches unavailable

ftbultimine.ftbultimine.server_settings.features.sin: When true, right-clicking a crop block without the Ultimine key held harvests it
ftbultimine.ftbultimine.server_settings.costs_limits:
The maximum number of blocks which can be broken or modified in one operation
If FTB Ranks is installed, the 'ftbultimine.max_blocks' node takes priority, if present

This can be a fractional amount
If FTB Ranks is installed, the 'ftbultimine.exhaustion_per_block' node takes priority, if present

If true, players must have equipped either a damageable tool or an item in the 'ftbultimine:tools' item tag
See also 'Require Valid Tool to Ultimine'

If true, the equipped tool must be valid for the block being ultimined,
i.e. it must be able to harvest dropped items
See also 'Require Tool to Ultimine'

This can be a fractional amount
If FTB Ranks is installed, the 'ftbultimine.experience_per_block' node takes priority, if present

Time in ticks required between successive uses of the Ultimine function
20 ticks = 1 second
If FTB Ranks is installed, the 'ftbultimine.ultimine_cooldown' node takes priority, if present
ftbultimine.ftbultimine.server_settings.misc.merge_t: For shapeless digging mode, any blocks in these tags which share a block tag with the original mined block are valid candidates for Ultimining.

Default of '*' means any block is a valid candidate.

functionalstorage.linkingtool.linkingmode.single.desc: Links a drawer to a controller
functionalstorage.linkingtool.use:
* Sneak + Right Click in the air to change modes or to clear frequency. 
* Left Click an Ender Drawer to store its Frequency. 
* Right Click in the air to change actions. 

Right click a controller to setup the tool then use it nearby drawers to link. 

While holding the tool it will show the connected drawers to the selected controller.

fusion.fusion.resource_packs.requires_newer_ver: (Made for a newer version of Fusion)
fusion.fusion.resource_packs.warning_screen.mes: This resource pack is made for a newer version of Fusion and may not work correctly with the currently installed version of Fusion.
fusion.fusion.resource_packs.warning_screen.con: Do you want to enable the resource pack anyway?

gateways.info.gateways.gate_pearl:
A Gate Pearl allows you to summon a Gateway by using it on a block.

It can only be used when the new Gateway would not be too close to another Gateway, based on the current spacing.
gateways.info.gateways.gate_pearl.2:
An open Gateway will fail in a burst of lightning if it cannot spawn a mob, any active mob walks too far away, or the wave timer elapses.

Rewards will spawn from the Gateway as they are obtained.
gateways.error.gateways.no_space: There's not enough space to open this Gateway
gateways.error.gateways.wave_failed: The Gateway imploded because there was not enough space to spawn the next wave
gateways.error.gateways.too_far: The Gateway imploded because a wave entity ran too far away
gateways.error.gateways.entity_discarded: The Gateway imploded because an entity was removed without being killed
gateways.error.gateways.no_nearby_player: The Gateway imploded because there was no player nearby
gateways.error.gateways.out_of_lives: The Gateway imploded because you ran out of lives

geore.geore.configuration.disablePistonPushFor: Disable Piston Push For Budding GeOre
Disable piston push for budding GeOre (Overrides the vanilla behavior of breaking the block upon being pushed) [Default: false]
geore.geore.configuration.generateQuartzInNeth: Generate Quartz In Nether GeOre [Default: true]
geore.geore.configuration.spyglassIntensity.to: The intensity of the spyglass color overlay (0.5 = 50%) [Default: 0.5]
geore.geore.configuration.title: GeOre Settings

Set to true if you want dropped items to also check above and below them, set to false if not ("false" is vanilla behavior).
The radius (in blocks) that dropped items should check around them for other dropped items to combine with ("0.5" is vanilla behavior).

hostilenetworks.item.hostilenetworks.fab_directive.desc2: Right-Click to copy, Shift-Right-Click to apply
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.gui.learner_empty.3: when placed in the Deep Learner.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.gui.learner_empty.5: In order to train the model
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.gui.learner_empty.6: you must deliver the killing blow.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.gui.name: Name
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.gui.upgrade_disabled: Models cannot be upgraded via kills.

hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.fail.model: Please insert a Data Model to begin the simulation
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.fail.faulty: Insufficient data in model. Please insert a basic model or better

Simulation progress paused
System energy levels critical

hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.msg.multiple_models: Multiple models (%s and %s) matched the target entity. This is a modpack configuration issue.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.msg.no_model: No known models exist for this specimen.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.glow_squid:
Deep in the ocean they stow.
Blessed by a luminescent glow.
Dare you take it as your own?
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.sheep:
Masters of color, and all things warm.
Usually held back, unless blessed by _jeb.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.iron_golem:
The mind of a pumpkin,
piloting a metal frame.
Defends the village,
but not quite tame.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.squid:
Found in water, aimlessly so.
Drifts around without a goal.
Some wish they would just go.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.mooshroom:
Is it a cow or a fungus?
Not scary, unless you think
about The Last of Us.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.chicken:
"Quack."
No, that's not right, try again.
"Cluck."
There we go.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.cow:
Provides milk while alive.
Provides meat while dead.
The duality of cows.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.rabbit:
The rabbit hops and hops and hops.
He does this even through the night.
Never fights, unless his type is Ninety-Nine.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.polar_bear:
A Bear from the Pole,
quite strange indeed.
He is a sight to behold,
yet he cannot read.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.bat:
A well known fact is that
some bats are vampiric.
Unfortunately, it's up to you to find out which.
Try not to die.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.hoglin:
A Hog of incredible size.
Lives in Hell, and can be zombified.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.snow_golem:
The mind of a pumpkin, yet again.
He probably couldn't count to ten.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.pig:
The pig is an interesting case.
Worse than a cow for drops,
and worse than a horse for riding.
Maybe the killing is kindness...
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.breeze:
An airy relative of the Blaze,
Breezes can only be found
within the confines of a Trial Chamber.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.blaze:
Bring buckets and watch in despair
as it evaporates, and everything is
on fire. You are on fire.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.creeper:
An inverse architect by trade.
Generally misunderstood.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.drowned:
An undead man who has
defied death again.
Somehow he learned to use a Trident.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.ender_dragon:
Lord of The End, yet keeps no hoard.
Destroy the crystals, break the cycle.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.enderman:
Friendly unless provoked.
Fooled by pumpkins.
Teleports short distances.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.ghast:
If you hear something that sounds
like a crying llama, you're probably
hearing a ghast.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.skeleton:
A formidable archer, but may be
running some sort of cheat engine.
A shield would be useful.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.slime:
"A Slime Haiku"
The bounce
bounce his bounce
squish
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.spider:
Nocturnal douchebags.
Drops strands of string for some reason.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.witch:
Affinity with potions and concoctions.
Maybe you should develop an antidote.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.wither_skeleton:
A charred skeleton.
Its bow did not survive the process.
Hunted for their skulls.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.wither:
Do not approach this enemy, run!
Too bad it isn't weak to the sun.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.zombie:
They moan in the moonlight.
Does not understand the need
for personal space.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.guardian:
Some sort of mutated pufferfish.
Beware the beam attack!
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.evoker:
A powerful spellcaster.
Knows the secrets of undeath.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.elder_guardian:
The largest of the ocean
creatures, it lives in great
underwater monuments.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.magma_cube:
What happens if we give lava life?
This, this is what happens.
That's why we don't do that.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.phantom:
Annoying little beasts who
insist you go to sleep.
The Insomnia Police.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.shulker:
A reinforced, teleporting cannon.
Makes a nice chest when slain.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.vindicator:
An axe-wielding maniac.
Probably advisable to keep a
safe distance.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.warden:
The slumbering lord of
an Ancient City.
He cannot see,
but he does not need to.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.zombified_piglin:
A poor nether-dweller turned
into a zombie.
Has a lot of gold, though.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.ars_wilden:
Protectors of The Wilds.
Their form depends on their home.
Oh, and one of them can summon wolves.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.mimic:
A chest with a hunger.
One you wish to plunder.
Be careful,
lest you be torn asunder.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.piglich:
He resides in The Other.
His health bar makes weak men shudder.
Pray he doesn't utter the words:
"Your Mother."
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.raven:
An ebony bird, full of lore.
Quoth the Raven "Nevermore."
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.alpha_yeti:
Leader of the Yeti Tribe.
The cave is his design.
I wonder if he'd take a bribe,
to let you skip the fight.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.carminite_golem:
A golem without a pumpkin
is like a castle without a king.
This one is fueled by dark magic.
It's quite a scary thing.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.death_tome:
All of those enchanted books
that you discarded?
This is what happens to them.
It's your fault, really.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.deer:
They gaze with judgement.
I'm not sure what you did,
but clearly they're annoyed.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.fire_beetle:
Why does the fire beetle
get its own data model?
It drops the same things
as a creeper...
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.twilight_giant:
Sky-dwellers.
They reflect our universe,
four times larger.
Perhaps you could ask for a pickaxe.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.twilight_goblin:
Small folk, adorned with
the metal of knights.
How they got it
is beyond my knowledge.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.helmet_crab:
When you leave helmets
lying around on beaches,
this is what happens.
You get crabs.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.hydra:
Each head slain causes
two to rise.
If only you had a weapon
of a larger size...
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.kobold:
"You no take candle!"
is the only phrase I've
heard a kobold say.
He doesn't even have one!
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.lich:
A death-defying wizard.
His protective shield is weak
to magical damage.
Panics once it breaks.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.minotaur:
Half man, Half cow.
Far less imposing than a centaur.
Moooves incredibly fast.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.naga:
This deadly serpent lives
in his own courtyard.
He will demolish his home
to end your life.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.redcap:
Simple goblin miners.
They just don't want to share.
The Twilight isn't that deep,
to be fair.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.skeleton_druid:
Apparently some skeletons
figured out that golden hoes
can channel nature magic.
I'm disappointed that they did it first.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.snow_queen:
Atop a frozen castle
is a frozen queen.
She wishes never to be seen.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.twilight_ice:
The Snow Queen went through
a great deal of effort to
bring ice to life.
The failed experiments still wander around.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.towerwood_borer:
What happens when you cross
termintes with carminite?
You get these little bastards.
They can eat towerwood!
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.ur_ghast:
Quite possibly the largest
specimen I have ever seen.
Could you hope to slay it?
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.winter_wolf:
Pack hunters who thrive
in the snow.
Surprisingly, they have the same fur
as a Yeti.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.twilight_wraith:
The specter of a long-dead king.
Seeks to prevent you from taking
his things.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.yeti:
A lame, normal Yeti.
Wishes he was cool, like
the Alpha Yeti.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.basalz:
The much bulkier cousin
of our flammable friend.
Bestowed with earthen strength.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.blitz:
A wind elemental
by another name.
Fortunately, they lack control
of thunder.
hostilenetworks.hostilenetworks.trivia.blizz:
Take a Blaze
and remove the heat.
The resulting husk is
the icy Blizz.

industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.tooltip.energy_: Right Click into Energy Field Provider to link
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.tooltip.no_with: It seems that you can't place this, maybe a machine can do it.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.tooltip.accepts: Will use fluids from tanks placed on top of the mahcine
industrialforegoing.__comment2: @PAGE@" -> Will create a new page and the following text will be in a new page
industrialforegoing.__comment3: {" -> Will start a bold/colored string to be highlighted
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.title: Industrial Foregoing's Manual
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.landing: IF you need to automate all the things!
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.sewer: When provided with power it will collect {Sewage} from the animals in top.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.animal_gro: When provided with power and {breeding} food, it will feed baby animals to make them {grow} faster.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.animal_ind: When provided with power it will move {baby} animals from the back to the front.@L@@L@If {Adult Filter} is installed it will move adult animals instead of baby animals.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.animal_res: When provided with power it will try to interact animals with {buckets} and {shears} to milk cows and shear sheeps.@L@@L@It will also get {ink sacks} from squids.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.animal_sto: When provided with power and {food} for the animals it will feed them and put them in the mood. It needs the breeding item for the animal.\@L@\@L@There can only be {35} animals at the same time in the working area.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.biofuel_ge: Produces power when provided with Biofuel. A {bucket} of biofuel will produce 160RF/t, a total {640,000}RF. About {28} generators can be run from 1 {Bioreactor}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.bioreactor: When provided with {power} and {bio materials} will produce {biofuel}.@L@@L@Each item will increase the efficiency of all the items by {10}mb, at maximum efficiency, the reactor will produce {1440}mb consuming {9} different items.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.black_hole: It can hold 9 {Black Hole Units} and access its items. That means 9 times {2,147,483,647} of items.
It can can hold up to {2,147,483,647}mb of one fluid.
It can can hold up to {2,147,483,647} of one item.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.block_dest: When provided with power, it will {break} any block in front of it and move it to its inventory.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.block_plac: When provided with power, it will {place} in front of it the blocks in its inventory.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.crop_enric: When provided with power and fertilizer it will bonemeal crops and saplings.@L@@L@Accepted fertilizers: {bonemeal}, {Industrial Foregoing's} and {Forestry} fertilizer.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.crop_recol: When provided with power it will {harvest} fully grown crops and chop down trees, this includes pumpkins, melons and nether wart. Each operation will produce a bit of {sludge} that can be used in the {Sludge Refiner} to obtain some material. (Having the tank full will {not} slow the machine)
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.crop_sower: Plants seeds and saplings to be gathered in the {Plant Gatherer}. You can {lock} the slots by clicking in the lock and it will {filter} the inputs.@L@@L@The {colored} background represents the colored parts in the {top} of the block.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.dye_mixer: It will produce {dye} more efficiently that normal crafting.@L@@L@Giving it {red} dye, {green} dye and {blue} dye will fill the color buffers. When given a {Laser Lens} will focus that color and produce the specified dye.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.enchantmen: When provided with power it will act like an {anvil} but instead of player experience levels it will use {Essence} to achieve it.
When provided with power, a {book} and a {enchanted} item it will remove {1} enchantment from the item and apply it to the book.@L@@L@The enchanted item won't be damaged in the process.
When provided with power, an {enchanteable} item and {Essence} it will enchant the item just like an {enchanting table} does.@L@@L@It will use {3} buckets of Essence to do a {30} level enchant.
When provided with power and {any} item it will {sort} enchanted items from non enchanted items.@L@@L@Enchanted items will go to the {top} row and the non enchanted ones will go to the {bottom} row.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.energy_fie: The {Energy Field Addon} is an item that will go into the addon slots of any working machine. It will give {power} to the machines from its internal buffer.@L@@L@The {Energy Field Provider} will charge the internal buffer of the addons that are {linked} to it and are in its {range}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.fluid_craf: The {fluid crafter} will craft anything that needs {one} fluid type instead of using the {buckets} in the recipe. To make it work you need to put the items in the grid and {lock it}. After that you can supply the items in the grid and the fluid in the tank.@L@@L@If the recipe has multiple of one item you can use {Item Splitter} to use it easier.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.fluid_pump: When placed above a fluid, the Fluid Pump will take that fluid as a {filter} and begin to drain all fluids of the same type in the working area, it will search all the fluids {below} the fluids in the working area and drain them too. @L@@L@All the drained fluids will be replaced with {cobblestone}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.hydrator: When provided with power it will increase the {grow speed} of the crops in the working area.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.item_split: It will {split} the defined stack size on each slot of the allowed sides of the {output}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.laser_base: When fully charged with {Laser Drills} it will produce ores based on depth and biome.@L@@L@You can increase the chance of producing an ore by adding {Laser Lens} to it.@L@@L@You can decrease the chance of an ore by using an {Laser Lens (Inverted)}, that will increase the chance of the other ores a bit.@L@@L@{Check JEI for chances}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.laser_dril: When placed {1} block away of a {Laser Base} it will charge it with power.@L@@L@If it is wrongly placed it will have a name tag on top telling you so.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.latex_proc: When provided with {power}, {water} and {latex} will produce tiny dry rubber.@L@@L@It will consume {75}mb of latex and {1000}mb of water to produce {1} tiny dry rubber.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.lava_fabri: When provided with large amounts of power, it will produce {lava}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.material_s: When provided with power it will start generating {cobblestone}. It has {4} operations that the machine can do. It can {smelt} items, {2x2} craft them, it can {grind} them and it can {3x3} craft them.@L@@L@For example, the first slot has cobble and the first operation is 'Smelt', then the cobble will turn into stone, the second operation is 2x2 craft, then the stone will turn into stone bricks, etc.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.mob_detect: It will emit a {Redstone Signal} to the back depending on the amount of entities in front of it.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.mob_duplic: When provided with power, {Essence} and any entity in a {Mob Imprisonment Tool} it will spawn them around it.@L@@L@It will {count} the entities nearby and stop spawning if there are too many.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.mob_reloca: When provided with power it will {kill} any entity in front of it as if a player would.@L@@L@It will {collect} the dropped items and it will transform the experience orbs into {Essence}". When having the {Adult Filter Addon} it will kill only adult mobs.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.mob_slaugh: When provided with power it will {grind} any entity in front of it and produce {Liquid Meat} from it.@L@@L@It will {not} drop items or experience in the process.@L@@L@It will also produce {some pink} slime in the process.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.orediction: It will transform items with the same {oredicted} entry into the filtered item.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.ore_proces: It will break down {Silk Touched} ores into its drops.@L@@L@Providing it with {Essence} break them with fortune.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.petrified_: When provided with any {solid fuel} will produce power. The {RF}/tick is decided from its burn time, the more {burn time} the fuel has the more {RF}/tick it will produce.@L@@L@NOTE: All fuels burn the same time.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.potion_ene: When provided with power it will act like an advanced version of the {brewing stand}.@L@@L@First of all it will {fill} glass bottles with water then it will start {brewing} all the ingredients in the top row in {order}, from left to right.@L@@L@Follow the {arrow} to check the current operation.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.protein_ge: "Produces power when provided with Protein. A {bucket} of protein will produce 320RF/t, a total {1,280,000}RF.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.protein_re: When provided with {power} and {some animals drops} will produce {protein}.@L@@L@Each item will increase the efficiency of all the items by {10}mb, at maximum efficiency, the reactor will produce {1440}mb consuming {9} different items.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.resourcefu: It acts like a normal {furnace} but it can burn {3} items at a time and will produce a bit of {Essence} each time a item is smelted.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.sewage_com: When provided with power and {2} buckets of Sewage, it will solidify it into {Fertilizer}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.sludge_ref: When provided with power and {1} bucket of Sludge (Produced in the Plant Gatherer), it will produce {earth like} materials.@L@@L@{If JEI is installed look the uses of the Sludge Refiner to check the chance of the produced materials}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.spores_rec: When provided with power, {1} bucket of water and a {mushroom} of any kind it will grow them internally.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.tree_fluid: When placed in front of any {log} it will produce {Liquid Latex}.@L@@L@It will slowly {consume} the log, each Tree Fluid Extractor contributes to the consuming of the log.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.villager_t: When provided with power and a {Mob Imprisonent Tool} with a {Villager} in it, will give you access to automatically trade with the villager.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.water_cond: When having {2} of more sides with water sources blocks it will start collecting water.@L@@L@The more sides with source blocks the more water it will produce.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.water_reso: When placed over a pool of {3x3} pool of water it will automatically {fish}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.wither_bui: When provided with power, {3} Wither Skulls and {4} soulsand, it will completely build the wither spawning structure in the working area.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.welcome_ma: Welcome to Industrial Foregoing's Manual!@L@@L@To get started you need to place a Tree Fluid Extractor in front of a log to collect Latex and pump into Latex Processing Unit with some water to get Tiny Rubber. @L@@L@{NOTE: Machines don't auto eject, neither they pull! Machines accept RF, FE, Tesla and Mek power.}
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.mob_impris: When right clicked an entity with the item in the hand it will {hold} the entity inside it.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.meat_feede: When the Meat Feeder is in your {inventory} and has some {Liquid Meat} in it, will automatically {feed} you.@L@@L@It acts like a normal fluid container.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.range_addo: It will increase the working {range} of some machines. Not all of the machines will accept them.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.straw: It allows you to {drink} in world fluids or in tanks. It can cause different {effects} depending on the fluid.@L@@L@@L@@L@@L@(I'm not responsible of the bad effects that the straw can cause)
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.pink_slime: Can be obtained by killing a {Pink Slime} that will spawned when placing some {Pink Slime Fluid} in the world.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.upgrades: Industrial Foregoing doesn't have speed upgrades but you can used {Energy Upgrades} and {Speed Upgrades} from Tesla Core Lib to speed up machines. They need the previous tier to be installed to be able to add the next one.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.plant_inte: When provided with power, it will {right click} crops and fruits to harvest them and store them in its inventory or drop them in the ground if they can't be stored. @L@@L@{*It works with Pam's Fruits and Plants by default.}
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.transfer_a: Transfer addons come in all the shapes and forms but only can transfer {items} and {fluids}, and they can pull and they can push.@L@@L@To change the direction of the action simply {right click} it to cycle between the directions or {shift right click} a side of a block to select that direction. You can enchant the addon with {Efficiency} to increase its transfer amount.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.fluiddicti: This machine will transform similar fluids from different mods. You can check {JEI} for possible recipes. You simply need to click on the buttons to select the fluid.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.conveyors: Conveyors will {push} any entites to the direction they are facing. They come on different colors and verticality.@L@@L@They can be upgraded by right-clicking them with {glowstone dust} and the will move entities faster. Right-clicking them with {plastic} will prevent the items from being picked up.@PAGE@It also has a different {upgrades} that can be attached to the sides or the center of the conveyor. They will be placed on the {further} side of the conveyor. Upgrades can be removed by sneak + right clicking on it.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.conveyor_u: The extraction upgrade will try to {extract} items from the inventories that are touching the upgrade and place them inside of the conveyor. @L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist and it can be upgraded with {glowstone dust} to speed up the extaction. (It will stop extracting if it extracted too many items that aren't picked up)

The insertion upgrade will try to {insert} items that are touching the upgrade into the inventory touching the upgrade side. @L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist for the insertion. The {range} can be increased in the GUI so it will pick up all the items in the conveyor instead of the items just touching the upgrade.

The detection upgrade is placed in the center part of the conveyor and will emmit a {redstone} signal when triggered by an entity.@L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist and you can filter mobs by using a {Mob Imprisonment Tool} with an entity in it. The {redstone control} can be inverted in the GUI.

The bouncing upgrade is placed in the center part of the conveyor and it will {launch} entites in the air in the direction the conveyor is facing.@L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist and you can filter mobs by using a {Mob Imprisonment Tool} with an entity in it. The {velocity} values (horizontal and vertical) can be changed in the GUI.

The dropping upgrade is placed in the center part of the conveyor and it will {drop} entities directly below it or insert items in inventories below it.@L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist and you can filter mobs by using a {Mob Imprisonment Tool} with an entity in it.

The blinking upgrade is placed in the center part of the conveyor and it will {teleport} entities in a short range in front of the conveyor. @L@@L@It has a {filter} with whitelist or blacklist and you can filter mobs by using a {Mob Imprisonment Tool} with an entity in it. The teleport {distance} can be configured in the GUI.

The splitting upgrade will {distribute} entities evenly, or with the configured ratio, to the side the upgrade is placed.@L@@L@The more {ratio} it has the more entities will go that way. The ratio can be configured in the GUI.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.froster: The froster allows you to create cold substances by spinning the supplied {water} at very fast speeds and reducing the temperature using {power}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.ore_washer: When provided with {liquid meat} and an {ore} it will wash the ore bits with meat into {raw ore meat} for further processing.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.ore_fermen: When provided with {raw ore meat} it will be processed into {fermented ore meat} and growing it's size. It needs a {hot source}(hot fluids, fire, magma blocks) on the bottom and a {cold fluid}(cold fluids, ice, ...) in any other side.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.ore_sieve: When provided with {fermented ore meat} and a little bit of {sand}, it will filter the ore from the meat and transform it into {dust}.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.fortune_ad: It will add {fortune/looting} to the machines that accept the addon. It needs to be enchanted with {Fortune} and it will use the enchantment level for the machine actions.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.pitiful_fu: Works the same as the Petrified Fuel Generator but it only accepts {wood} stuf.
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.book.infinity_d: A powerful drill that has lost all of it's power. By recharging it will allow you to destroy worlds at a bigger rates. The more power it has, the bigger areas it can mine. In order to preserve that energy it can use {Biofuel} at a more efficient rate. Shift+Right Clicking it will allow you to change the mining area. @L@@L@{It holds secrets that can be easily achieved by Patreons and some rare mobs in the world.}
industrialforegoing.tooltip.industrialforegoing.enchantment_: Enchantments from items will be transfered to books
Enchantments from items will be transformed into essence (No Books required)
Mobs won't generate essence but each kill will
have a random chance to use Looting at any level
industrialforegoing.tooltip.industrialforegoing.backpack.pic: Sneak + Open Backpack Key to swap modes
Ctrl + Open Backpack Key to disable
industrialforegoing.conveyor.upgrade.industrialforegoing.too: Inserting items in all the conveyor
industrialforegoing.industrialforegoing.subtitle.nuke_explos: Embrace Infinity Nuke's death and destruction
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.machine_setting: * Sneak Right Click to clear the copied settings
* Sneak Right Click a machine to copy the machine settings
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.proxy.client.al: Press Alt + F4 to cheat this item
industrialforegoing.text.industrialforegoing.donation.author: author of these mods in this pack:
industrialforegoing.tooltip.industrialforegoing.hydroponic.f: * Use in the Hydroponic Bed to gather Crop Data
* Use in the Simulated Hydroponic Bed to get drops

inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.title: Inventory Essentials
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.forceClientIm: Use the client implementation even on servers that have the mod installed - only useful for development purposes.
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.allowBulkTran: Allow Transfer All on Empty Slot
Should Transfer All move all items even if an empty slot was clicked?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.bulkTransferA: Should space-clicking armor in the inventory swap to all matching armor?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.enableSingleT: Should ctrl-clicking only move one item at a time instead of the full stack?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.enableBulkTra: Should shift-ctrl-clicking move all items of the same type at once?
Should control-space-clicking an item move one of each item from that inventory at once?
Should space-clicking an item move all items from that inventory at once?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.enableBulkDro: Should shift-ctrl-drop-clicking drop all items of the same type at once?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.enableShiftDr: Should holding shift and moving your mouse over items quick-transfer them without requiring each to be clicked?
inventoryessentials.config.inventoryessentials.enableMiddleC: Should middle-clicking a slot sort the inventory?

iris.iris.sodium.failure.title: Iris failed to load!
iris.iris.sodium.failure.reason.notFound: Iris requires Sodium for good performance, but Sodium is not installed. Please download Sodium, place it in your mods folder, and re-launch the game.
iris.iris.sodium.failure.reason.incompatible: Iris requires Sodium for good performance, but an incompatible version was found. Please download the correct version of Sodium, place it in your mods folder, remove the incorrect version, and re-launch the game.
iris.iris.nec.failure.title: [%s] Not Enough Crashes detected!
iris.iris.nec.failure.description:
Not Enough Crashes can severely break the game while handling crashes, and does not provide accurate results.
A good alternative is MixinTrace, which can identify the cause of a crash more reliably and does not put the game in an invalid state.
iris.iris.unsupported.pack.description: The shaderpack you are trying to load contains features unsupported by %s. Please try another pack. List %s
iris.iris.unsupported.pack.macos: macOS is known to have issues with many shader packs.

iris.options.iris.shadowDistance.enabled: Allows you to change the maximum distance for shadows. Terrain and entities beyond this distance will not cast shadows. Lowering the shadow distance can significantly increase performance.
iris.options.iris.shadowDistance.disabled: Your current shader pack has already set a render distance for shadows; you cannot change it.
iris.options.iris.shadowDistance.sodium_toolt: The shadow render distance controls how far away terrain can potentially be rendered in the shadow pass. Lower distances mean that less terrain will be rendered, improving frame rates. This option cannot be changed on packs which explicitly specify a shadow render distance. The actual shadow render distance is capped by the View Distance setting.
iris.options.iris.colorSpace.sodium_tooltip: The color space to transform the screen to. Works on top of shader packs. Use SRGB if unsure.

ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.xmas_right_click: Right-click on furnace to wrap it as a gift!
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.xmas1: But won't the wrappings catch fire?
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.xmas2: Sneaking and right-clicking on a furnace opens the gift
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.spooky2: Sneaking and right-clicking on a furnace will cleanse any spookalizations
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.heater_not_bound: Has yet to be bound to an energy source!
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.heater_tip: Place in the fuel slot of the furnace
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.heater_tip1: Only works from Iron tier and above
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_speed_con: -Uses up twice the amount of fuel.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_xp: Experience granted from smelting will be ejected to nearby fluid tanks.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_xp_1: Experience granted from smelting will given to the player that placed the furnace.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_xp_switch: Sneak & Right click in the air to switch modes.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_fuel_pro: +Fuel heats up the furnace twice as much.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_fuel_con: -Slows down the cooktime by 25% for all recipes.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_factory_con: -Uses power for heat instead of fuel.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_generator_p: +Heat from the furnace will generate power.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_generator_c: -The furnace can no longer smelt items.
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.heater_block: Link with a Wireless Heat Receiver (done by placing the receiver in the Wireless Heat Transmitter)
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.heater_block1: Provide this block with Forge Energy and it will power all Wireless Heat Receivers that are bound
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_redstone_ri: Right-click in the air to change mode
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.augment_redstone_co: Redstone Control mode: will NOT smelt when supplied with a redstone signal
Redstone Inverse Control mode: will ONLY smelt when supplied with a redstone signal
Comparator mode: acts as an internal comparator, outputs the signal to all sides
Comparator Subtraction mode: same as Comparator mode but you can subtract with a signal strength that can be configured in the furnace GUI
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.rainbow_blowup: Something is telling me to blow it up...
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.rainbow_gen2: RF/tick if all other furnaces are generating power
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.rainbow_gen3: For power generation, one of each tier of furnace must be an active generator and not have a full buffer
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.rainbow_gen4: Outputs energy to any side that is configured as output, it wont add energy to the internal buffer
ironfurnaces.tooltip.ironfurnaces.rainbow_gen5: You can only have one Rainbow Generator
